message:
fix(resilience): replace panic-on-poison with graceful lock recovery across all concurrent components

Harden all Mutex and RwLock usage against lock poisoning by replacing
.expect("lock poisoned") patterns with unwrap_or_else(|p| p.into_inner())
recovery helpers. This prevents cascading panics when a thread panics while
holding a lock — instead the data is recovered and operation continues.

Components hardened:
- frankensearch-core/cache.rs (S3FifoCache): add lock_state() helper
- frankensearch-embed/batch_coalescer.rs (BatchCoalescer): add lock_state()
  helper across submit/wait/try_form_batch/shutdown/pending_count paths
- frankensearch-fusion/adaptive.rs (AdaptiveFusion): add lock_state()
  helper across blend_factor/rrf_k/update/reset/snapshot paths
- frankensearch-fusion/cache.rs (IndexCache): add read_index()/write_index()
  helpers for RwLock with warn-level tracing on recovery
- frankensearch-fusion/queue.rs (EmbeddingQueue): add lock_state() helper
  plus defensive get_mut checks and recovery logging in submit/drain/requeue
- frankensearch-storage/pipeline.rs (InMemoryVectorSink): inline recovery
  with tracing in persist() and entries() methods
- frankensearch-index/simd.rs: minor formatting cleanup

Each component includes a new regression test that intentionally poisons
the lock and verifies all public API paths remain functional afterward.
Beads metadata updated (bd-30d2.17-23 closed).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
